title: acer italum subsp. hyrcanum
intro: Acer italum subsp. hyrcanum, also known as the Iranian maple, is a subspecies of deciduous tree that belongs to the Acer genus. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to contribute to the treatment of various health conditions. The bark and leaves of Acer italum subsp. hyrcanum have been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, and have been reported to offer health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Acer italum subsp. hyrcanum can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, infusions, and extracts, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
